title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Harriet Lymon (formerly Harriet Lyman) informs Mrs. Oliver that she and a man named James Jenkins witnessed Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith sign an addition to her will, confirming the signature is authentic. Lymon notes that Jenkins moved to Australia a year and a half ago and is no longer local. Lymon expresses guilt for remaining silent about the will and mentions that a child named Joey witnessed Miss Olga killing Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. Lymon observes that Miss Olga appeared smug regarding a potential inheritance but fled suddenly when the police arrived. Mrs. Oliver advises Lymon to tell this information to Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith's lawyer and promises to speak for her.

Later, Hercule Poirot joins Mrs. Oliver. While discussing the case, Poirot realizes the importance of "dates," noting that the roots of the present murder lie in a past event. He explains that a murder occurring years ago, witnessed by a girl, led to that girl being killed four days ago. Poirot mentions that his investigation has yielded facts arranged by date and notes that no one believes the testimony of Joyce Reynolds. Poirot also discusses the possibility of a forged will, suggesting that while people suspect the foreign exchange student, there was another forger in the village. This forger, whom Poirot does not know, died approximately two years ago after being killed by a knife following a dispute involving jealousy and a girl. Poirot suggests that these seemingly independent incidents may be interconnected.
